words.definition: Not often; rarely
words.example: He seldom goes to the movies.

words.definition: Made up of various parts; a combination of different elements
words.example: The composite image was created from several photos.

wordFamily.example: The composition of the paint is complex.

words.definition: Incapable of producing any useful result; pointless
words.example: It was futile to try to convince him.

wordFamily.example: He realized the futility of arguing.

words.definition: Consist of; be made up of

wordFamily.example: The group is comprised of experts.

words.definition: Bring or recall to the conscious mind
words.example: The song evoked memories of childhood.

wordFamily.example: The evocation of past events was powerful.

words.definition: Afraid or anxious
words.example: The frightened child hid under the bed.

wordFamily.example: The loud noise gave me a fright.

words.definition: A brief or partial view; to see briefly
words.example: I caught a glimpse of the sunrise.

wordFamily.example: She got a glimpse of the future.
He glimpsed her in the crowd.

words.definition: A sudden sharp pain or emotional pang
words.example: He felt a twinge of guilt.

wordFamily.example: She experienced a twinge in her back.

words.definition: Able to be separated or divided
words.example: The two issues are not separable.

words.definition: Able to be connected or related to each other
Suggest or assume the existence of something as a basis for reasoning
words.example: He postulated that the earth revolves around the sun.

words.definition: Formally declare one's abandonment of a claim, right, or possession

wordFamily.example: His renunciation of wealth was surprising.

words.definition: Come before in time, order, or position
words.example: The appetizer preceded the main course.

wordFamily.example: There is no precedent for this.

words.definition: Allowing freedom, especially in behavior
A widely held but oversimplified image of a group
words.example: The stereotype that all artists are poor is false.

words.definition: An introduction to a book; to begin or introduce
words.example: He prefaced his speech with a joke.

words.definition: In agreement or harmony; matching
words.example: His actions were congruent with his words.

wordFamily.example: There is congruence between the reports.

words.definition: Organize and carry out; behavior
The scope or extent of something's influence or operation
words.example: This matter falls within the purview of the committee.

words.definition: A heavy object that holds a ship in place; to secure firmly
words.example: The anchor kept the boat steady.

wordFamily.example: Anchor the shelf to the wall.

words.definition: State a fact or belief confidently and forcefully
Make someone or something free of something unwanted
words.example: He wanted to rid himself of debt.

words.definition: Holding traditional values; cautious about change
words.example: He has conservative views on marriage.

words.definition: Accept or act in accordance with; tolerate
words.example: He must abide by the rules.

words.definition: The extreme limit of settled land; a boundary
Clever at being deceptive; skill in achieving goals through deceit
words.example: The cunning fox escaped the trap.

words.definition: Keep in subservience; burden with cruel or unjust treatment
Forcibly put an end to; prevent from being expressed
Move something into position to block an opening
Break into many pieces; destroy completely
words.example: The glass shattered on the floor.

words.definition: Completely engrossing; holding attention firmly
Something that has been left out or excluded
words.example: The omission of his name was an accident.

words.definition: A range of different things, often on a scale
words.example: Opinions span the entire political spectrum.

words.definition: A firm hold; to grasp tightly
words.example: He tightened his grip on the rope.

words.definition: Seize and hold firmly; understand
Very good, excellent (British informal)
words.example: It was a ripping good yarn.

wordFamily.example: There's a rip in my shirt.

words.definition: The action of arousing interest, energy, or activity
Remonstrate with angrily; reprimand
words.example: The teacher scolded the noisy students.

words.definition: The action of frightening or overawing someone
words.example: They used intimidation to get what they wanted.

words.definition: Trying to destroy or undermine established authority

wordFamily.example: They tried to subvert the government.

words.definition: Sit, lie, or fall with limbs spread out
The possibility of a future event; a potential customer
words.example: The prospect of failure scared him.

words.definition: Prevent from having, using, or enjoying something
words.example: The prisoners were deprived of food.

words.definition: Encourage the development of; bring up a child
An agreement reached by mutual concession
Give a tangible form to an idea; represent
words.example: She embodies the spirit of the company.

wordFamily.example: He is the embodiment of kindness.

words.definition: Torment or torture physically or mentally
Become progressively worse
Voluntarily cease to keep or claim; give up
words.example: He relinquished control of the company.

words.definition: Shut forcefully and loudly; criticize harshly

wordFamily.example: The door closed with a slam.

words.definition: Something added to complete or enhance
To one side; a remark not intended to be heard by all
words.example: She whispered an aside to her friend.

words.definition: Look steadily and intently
Escape from someone or something; avoid
Very great or intense; showing deep insight
words.example: The lecture had a profound effect on me.

words.definition: Impossible to satisfy
words.example: He has an insatiable appetite for knowledge.

words.definition: A strong, persistent desire
Stay in a place longer than necessary
Steal goods violently, especially in war
Think about carefully, especially before deciding
The action of making false spoken statements damaging to reputation
Demand or specify a requirement, typically in an agreement
words.example: The contract stipulates payment by Friday.

words.definition: An expression of disapproval or opposition
words.example: He raised an objection to the plan.

words.definition: Treat as insignificant or peripheral
Keep a thought or feeling in mind; a safe haven
Impose a misleading belief upon someone
words.example: Don't delude yourself into thinking it's easy.

words.definition: Painful or laborious effort
words.example: The travail of writing a book.

words.definition: A loud and confused noise; to shout loudly
Write or carve words onto something
words.example: She inscribed her name in the book.

words.definition: Remove restraints; release from control
Having mixed feelings about someone or something
Experienced or done by each of two or more parties
Enjoy oneself in a luxurious way; take self-indulgent pleasure
words.example: He luxuriated in a hot bath.

words.definition: Burst or shatter violently
Showing no originality; blindly obedient
A polite expression of praise or admiration
Making something more entertaining or interesting
Gradually spread through or over
words.example: Her cheeks were suffused with color.

words.definition: Extreme physical or mental suffering
Express disapproval or disappointment
words.example: She looked at him with reproach.

words.definition: The action of making something smaller or less
Call upon someone to be present; gather courage
Use something to maximum advantage; power or influence
words.example: She used her experience as leverage.

words.definition: Average in amount or intensity; to make less extreme
Appear or claim to be or do something
words.example: The document purports to be official.

words.definition: A medicine or treatment; to correct a problem
An edge or border; be on the point of something
words.example: She was on the verge of tears.

words.definition: Travel across or through
Make secret information known
words.example: He refused to disclose the details.

words.definition: One behind another; working together
Devote effort to making oneself look attractive; feel self-satisfaction
A person overly concerned with minor details or rules
words.example: He's such a pedant about grammar.

words.definition: The state of being no longer needed or useful
Done, given, or acting of one's own free will
Change the natural qualities of something
Based on or in accordance with reason or logic
Eat greedily or ravenously
Act in accordance with a wish or command
words.example: You must comply with the rules.

words.definition: In an orderly, systematic way
